design_plan: rng=42; audience=Super vs Folk evaluators; AIDA: attention via centered hero, interest via fair Folk framing, desire via computer-use cache advantage, action via black pill CTA; hero math: max-width 1120px for 2–3 lines; density proof: compare-grid uses grid-auto-flow:dense; contrast check: black vs white pills; competitors label sweep complete.
headline: Super vs Folk — personal AI agents compared

site_brief.goal: Create a fair but persuasive competitor comparison page for Super vs Folk. Use supplied sources for current facts, avoid fake claims, and make the practical argument that Super is better suited for repeated computer-use work because its cache can lower repeated execution cost.
site_brief.title: Super vs Folk

summary: A fair, design-forward comparison for people evaluating Super and Folk. Folk fits lightweight, niche automation. Super is built for durable computer‑use workflows, with a reusable computer-use cache that makes repeated work faster and cheaper over time. The page also situates both tools within the broader agent landscape alongside Orchids, Siri, ChatGPT, Gemini, and Grok.
